steps.title: Frame the alternate name
steps.content: The phrase or SuperDARN, as space physicists like Nozomu Nishitani call it interrupts the main sentence to give another name for the network. A dash already closes that interruption after it, so the opening punctuation must match it.

steps.title: Match the paired punctuation
steps.content: Choice A uses a comma that does not pair with the closing dash. Choice B uses a colon, which cannot close with a dash. Choice C leaves the interrupting phrase without opening punctuation. The answer is D.
